Badge.name: Perfectionist
Badge.description: Completed all tasks in a moving plan

Badge.name: Pack Rat
Badge.description: Packed all boxes in a moving plan

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain TX DL within 90 days. Visit local DPS office. Vehicle inspection required within 90 days.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register by mail or in person. Deadline: 30 days before election. No online registration.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Deregulated electricity market — choose your provider at powertochoose.org.
StateRule.taxInfo: No state income tax. Property tax rates among highest in US (~1.8%). Sales tax: 6.25% + local.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must update DL within 10 days of establishing residency. Visit local DMV or apply online at dmv.ca.gov.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at registertovote.ca.gov. Deadline: 15 days before election (conditional registration available).
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: PG&E, SCE, SDG&E. Deregulated energy market in some areas.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 1%-13.3%. No tax on Social Security benefits. High property tax exemption for homeowners.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 15/30/5. Earthquake insurance optional but recommended.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain NY DL within 30 days. Visit local DMV. Vehicle inspection required annually.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at elections.ny.gov. Deadline: 25 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Con Edison, National Grid, NYSEG. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 4%-10.9%. NYC residents pay additional city tax. Property tax varies.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 25/50/10. No-fault state. PIP required.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain FL DL within 30 days. Visit local DHSMV office or apply at flhsmv.gov.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at registertovoteflorida.gov. Deadline: 29 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: FPL, Duke Energy, TECO. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: No state income tax. Sales tax: 6% + local. Homestead exemption up to $50K for property tax.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance: PIP ($10K) and property damage ($10K) required. Bodily injury recommended.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain NJ DL within 60 days. Visit MVC. Vehicle inspection every 2-4 years.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at voter.svrs.nj.gov. Deadline: 21 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: PSE&G, JCP&L, NJ American Water. Deregulated electricity market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 1.4%-10.75%. Sales tax: 6.625%. Property tax highest in US.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 15/30/5. No-fault state. PIP required.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain PA DL within 60 days. Visit PennDOT office. Emissions test in select counties. Annual safety inspection.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at vote.pa.gov. Deadline: 15 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: PECO, PPL, Columbia Gas. Deregulated electricity market.
StateRule.taxInfo: Flat state income tax: 3.07%. Sales tax: 6% (7-8% in some counties). Local wage taxes common.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 15/30/5. No-fault state.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain IL DL within 90 days. Visit Secretary of State office. Vehicle emissions test in Chicago metro.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at ova.elections.il.gov. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: ComEd, Ameren, Nicor Gas. Deregulated electricity market.
StateRule.taxInfo: Flat state income tax: 4.95%. Property tax among highest in US. Sales tax: 6.25% + local.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 25/50/20. Uninsured motorist coverage recommended.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain GA DL within 30 days. Visit DDS office. Emissions test required in metro Atlanta counties.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at registertovote.sos.ga.gov. Deadline: 29 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Georgia Power, Atlanta Gas Light. Deregulated natural gas market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 1%-5.49%. Sales tax: 4% + local. Homestead exemption available.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain WA DL within 30 days. Visit DOL office. Emissions test in select counties.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at sos.wa.gov/elections. Same-day registration. All-mail voting.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Puget Sound Energy, Seattle City Light. Regulated. Low rates (hydropower).
StateRule.taxInfo: No state income tax. Sales tax: 6.5% + local (up to ~10%). B&O tax for businesses.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain CO DL within 30 days. Visit county DMV office. Emissions testing required in Denver metro.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at govotecolorado.gov. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Xcel Energy. Partially deregulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: Flat state income tax: 4.4%. Property tax relatively low. Sales tax: 2.9% + local.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain AL DL within 30 days. Visit county license office. Title and registration at county probate office.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at alabamavotes.gov. Deadline: 15 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Alabama Power, Alagasco. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 2%-5%. Property tax rates among lowest in US. Sales tax: 4% + local.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain AK DL within 90 days. Visit local DMV. Vehicle registration required within 10 days.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at voterregistration.alaska.gov. Deadline: 30 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Chugach Electric, ENSTAR. High energy costs due to remote location.
StateRule.taxInfo: No state income tax. No state sales tax (some local taxes). PFD (Permanent Fund Dividend) paid annually.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 50/100/25. Higher minimums than most states.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain AZ DL immediately upon establishing residency. Visit MVD or third-party provider. DL valid until age 65.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at azsos.gov/elections. Deadline: 29 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: APS, SRP. Regulated market. Solar incentives available.
StateRule.taxInfo: Flat state income tax: 2.5%. Property tax varies by county. Sales tax: 5.6% + local.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain AR DL within 30 days. Visit local revenue office. Title transfer within 30 days.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register by mail or in person. Deadline: 30 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Entergy Arkansas, CenterPoint Energy. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 2%-4.7%. Sales tax: 6.5% + local.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain CT DL within 30 days. Visit DMV office. Emissions test required for registration.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at voterregistration.ct.gov. Deadline: 7 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Eversource, United Illuminating. Regulated market with high rates.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 3%-6.99%. Property tax among highest in US. Sales tax: 6.35%.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain DE DL within 60 days. Visit DMV location. No vehicle inspection required.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at ivote.de.gov. Deadline: 24 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Delmarva Power, Chesapeake Utilities. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 2.2%-6.6%. No sales tax. Low property tax.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 25/50/10. PIP required.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain HI DL within 30 days. Visit county DMV. Safety inspection required annually.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at elections.hawaii.gov. All-mail voting state since 2020.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Hawaiian Electric (HECO). Highest electricity rates in US.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 1.4%-11%. GET tax: 4% (instead of sales tax).
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 20/40/10. No-fault state. PIP required.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain ID DL immediately upon establishing residency. Visit county assessor office. No emissions test.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at idahovotes.gov. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Idaho Power, Intermountain Gas. Regulated market. Low electricity rates.
StateRule.taxInfo: Flat state income tax: 5.8%. Sales tax: 6%. Property tax relatively low.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain IN DL within 60 days. Visit BMV branch. Emissions test in Lake and Porter counties.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at indianavoters.in.gov. Deadline: 29 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Duke Energy Indiana, Indiana Michigan Power, Vectren. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: Flat state income tax: 3.05% + county tax. Sales tax: 7%. Property tax capped at 1-3%.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain IA DL within 30 days. Visit county treasurer or DOT office. No emissions test.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at sos.iowa.gov. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: MidAmerican Energy, Alliant Energy. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 4.4%-6%. Sales tax: 6% + local.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain KS DL within 90 days. Visit county treasurer office. No emissions test.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at sos.ks.gov/elections. Deadline: 21 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Evergy, Kansas Gas Service. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 3.1%-5.7%. Sales tax: 6.5% + local.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 25/50/25. No-fault state. PIP required.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain KY DL within 30 days. Visit county clerk office. No emissions test.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at elect.ky.gov. Deadline: 29 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Kentucky Utilities, Louisville Gas & Electric. Regulated market. Low rates.
StateRule.taxInfo: Flat state income tax: 4.5%. Sales tax: 6%. Property tax relatively low.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 25/50/25. No-fault state. PIP required ($10K).

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain LA DL within 30 days. Visit OMV office or use expresslane.org.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at geauxvote.com. Deadline: 30 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Entergy Louisiana, Atmos Energy. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 1.85%-4.25%. Sales tax: 4.45% + local (combined can exceed 10%).
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 15/30/25. High insurance rates statewide.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain ME DL within 30 days. Visit BMV office. Annual vehicle inspection required.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at maine.gov/sos/cec. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Central Maine Power, Versant Power. Deregulated electricity market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 5.8%-7.15%. Sales tax: 5.5%. Homestead exemption available.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain MD DL within 60 days. Visit MVA office. Emissions test required in most counties.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at elections.maryland.gov. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: BGE, Pepco, Delmarva Power. Deregulated electricity market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 2%-5.75% + county tax. Sales tax: 6%. Property tax varies by county.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 30/60/15. PIP required.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain MA DL within 30 days. Visit RMV office. Annual vehicle inspection required.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at sec.state.ma.us/ovr. Deadline: 20 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Eversource, National Grid. Deregulated electricity market. High rates.
StateRule.taxInfo: Flat state income tax: 5% (+ 4% surtax on income over $1M). Sales tax: 6.25%.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 20/40/5. No-fault state. PIP required.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain MI DL immediately upon establishing residency. Visit SOS office (appointment required).
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at mvic.sos.state.mi.us. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: DTE Energy, Consumers Energy. Partially deregulated.
StateRule.taxInfo: Flat state income tax: 4.05%. Sales tax: 6%. Property tax varies.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance: No-fault state with reformed PIP. Liability minimum 50/100/10.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain MN DL within 60 days. Visit DVS office. No emissions test.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at sos.state.mn.us. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Xcel Energy, CenterPoint Energy. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 5.35%-9.85%. Sales tax: 6.875% + local.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 30/60/10. No-fault state. PIP required.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain MS DL within 60 days. Visit DPS office. No emissions test.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register by mail. Deadline: 30 days before election. No online registration.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Mississippi Power, Entergy Mississippi. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 0%-5% (first $10K exempt). Sales tax: 7%. Low property tax.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain MO DL within 30 days. Visit license office. Emissions test in St. Louis and KC metros.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at sos.mo.gov/elections. Deadline: 27 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Ameren Missouri, Evergy, Spire. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 2%-4.95%. Sales tax: 4.225% + local.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain MT DL within 60 days. Visit county treasurer or MVD. No vehicle inspection.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at sosmt.gov/elections. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: NorthWestern Energy. Deregulated electricity market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 1%-6.75%. No sales tax. Property tax varies.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain NE DL within 30 days. Visit county treasurer or DMV. No emissions test.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at sos.nebraska.gov/elections. Deadline: 18 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: OPPD, LES, MUD. Public power state — all electricity is publicly owned.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 2.46%-5.84%. Sales tax: 5.5% + local. Property tax relatively high.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain NV DL within 30 days. Visit DMV office. Emissions test in Clark and Washoe counties.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at nvsos.gov. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: NV Energy. Partially deregulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: No state income tax. Sales tax: 6.85% + local. Low property tax.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain NH DL within 60 days. Visit DMV office. Annual vehicle inspection required.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register on election day at the polls. No advance deadline required.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Eversource, Liberty Utilities. Deregulated electricity market.
StateRule.taxInfo: No state income tax on wages. No sales tax. Property tax among highest in US.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance NOT mandatory (but financial responsibility required). Recommended: 25/50/25.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain NM DL within 30 days. Visit MVD office. Emissions test in Bernalillo County.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at portal.sos.state.nm.us. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: PNM, New Mexico Gas Company. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 1.7%-5.9%. Gross receipts tax: 5.125% + local.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain NC DL within 60 days. Visit NCDMV office. Annual vehicle inspection required.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at ncsbe.gov. Deadline: 25 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Duke Energy NC, Dominion Energy, Piedmont Natural Gas. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: Flat state income tax: 4.5%. Sales tax: 4.75% + local.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain ND DL within 60 days. Visit DOT office. No vehicle inspection.
StateRule.voterRegistration: No voter registration required — bring valid ID to vote.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Xcel Energy, Montana-Dakota Utilities. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 0%-2.5%. Sales tax: 5% + local. Low property tax.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 25/50/25. No-fault state.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain OH DL within 30 days. Visit BMV deputy registrar. Emissions test in select counties.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at olvr.ohiosos.gov. Deadline: 30 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Ohio Edison, AEP Ohio, Duke Energy, Columbia Gas. Deregulated electricity.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 0%-3.5% (first $26K exempt). Sales tax: 5.75% + local.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain OK DL within 30 days. Visit tag agency. Vehicle must be registered within 30 days.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at ok.gov/elections. Deadline: 25 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: OG&E, PSO. Regulated market. Low electricity rates.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 0.25%-4.75%. Sales tax: 4.5% + local. Low property tax.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain OR DL within 30 days. Visit DMV office. No vehicle inspection.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at sos.oregon.gov/voting. Deadline: 21 days. All-mail voting state.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Portland General Electric, Pacific Power, NW Natural. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 4.75%-9.9%. No sales tax. Property tax capped.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 25/50/20. PIP required.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain RI DL within 30 days. Visit DMV office. No emissions test.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at vote.ri.gov. Deadline: 30 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Rhode Island Energy. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 3.75%-5.99%. Sales tax: 7%. Property tax varies.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain SC DL within 90 days. Visit SCDMV office. No vehicle inspection.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at scvotes.gov. Deadline: 30 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Duke Energy, Dominion Energy. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 0%-6.4%. Sales tax: 6% + local. Property tax relatively low.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain SD DL within 90 days. Visit DPS office. No vehicle inspection.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at sdsos.gov. Deadline: 15 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Black Hills Energy, NorthWestern Energy. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: No state income tax. Sales tax: 4.5% + local. Property tax moderate.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain TN DL within 30 days. Visit county clerk office. Emissions test in select counties.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at ovr.govote.tn.gov. Deadline: 30 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Nashville Electric, Memphis Light Gas & Water, TVA. Regulated. Low rates.
StateRule.taxInfo: No state income tax on wages. Sales tax: 7% + local (combined up to 9.75%).

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain UT DL within 60 days. Visit DLD office. Emissions test in select counties.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at vote.utah.gov. Same-day registration available. All-mail voting.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Rocky Mountain Power, Dominion Energy Utah. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: Flat state income tax: 4.65%. Sales tax: 6.1% + local. Property tax relatively low.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 25/65/15. PIP required ($3K).

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain VT DL within 60 days. Visit DMV office. Annual vehicle inspection required.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at olvr.vermont.gov. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Green Mountain Power. Over 99% renewable electricity.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 3.35%-8.75%. Sales tax: 6% + local. Property tax relatively high.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain VA DL within 60 days. Visit DMV office. Annual safety inspection required.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at vote.virginia.gov. Deadline: 22 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Dominion Energy, Appalachian Power. Partially deregulated.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 2%-5.75%. Sales tax: 5.3% + local. Property tax varies by locality.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain WV DL within 30 days. Visit DMV regional office. No emissions test.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at ovr.sos.wv.gov. Deadline: 21 days before election.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Appalachian Power, MonPower. Regulated market. Low rates.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 2.36%-5.12%. Sales tax: 6% + local. Property tax relatively low.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain WI DL within 60 days. Visit DMV office. No vehicle inspection.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at myvote.wi.gov. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: We Energies, Alliant Energy, Madison Gas & Electric. Regulated market.
StateRule.taxInfo: State income tax: 3.54%-7.65%. Sales tax: 5% + county. Property tax moderate.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain WY DL immediately upon establishing residency. Visit county clerk. No vehicle inspection.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register on election day or in advance at county clerk. No online registration.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Black Hills Energy, Rocky Mountain Power. Regulated. Low rates.
StateRule.taxInfo: No state income tax. Sales tax: 4% + local. Property tax low.

StateRule.dmvRules: Must obtain DC DL within 30 days. Visit DC DMV office. Vehicle inspection required every 2 years.
StateRule.voterRegistration: Register online at dcboe.org. Same-day registration available.
StateRule.utilityInfo: Major providers: Pepco, Washington Gas. Deregulated electricity market.
StateRule.taxInfo: Income tax: 4%-10.75%. Sales tax: 6% (10% meals, 14.95% hotels). Property tax varies.
StateRule.insuranceRules: Auto insurance required: liability minimum 25/50/10. No-fault district.

Welcome to ReloTrack!

Hi {{firstName}},

Welcome to ReloTrack! We're excited to help you manage your relocation with ease.

  1. Add your current address
  2. Connect your services (utilities, insurance, etc.)
  3. Create a moving plan when you're ready
Go to Dashboard

ReloTrack · Smart relocation management

HelpArticle.title: Getting Started with ReloTrack
HelpArticle.content:
# Getting Started with ReloTrack

Welcome to ReloTrack! Here's how to get started:

## 1. Add Your Address
Start by adding your current home address. Go to **Addresses** and click **Add Address**. Fill in your street, city, state, and ZIP code.

## 2. Connect Services
Once you have an address, add the services connected to it — utilities, insurance, subscriptions, etc. Go to **Services** and click **Add Service**.

## 3. Create a Moving Plan
When you're ready to move, create a **Moving Plan** with your from/to addresses and move date. ReloTrack will generate a checklist of tasks.

## 4. Track Your Budget
Use the **Budget** section to track monthly income and expenses across all your services.

## 5. Explore More
- **Documents**: Upload and organize important files
- **Community**: Read and write reviews for service providers
- **Badges**: Earn achievements as you use ReloTrack
HelpArticle.excerpt: Learn how to set up your account and start managing your relocation.

HelpArticle.title: How to Add Your First Address
HelpArticle.content:
# How to Add Your First Address

## Steps
1. Navigate to **Addresses** from the sidebar
2. Click the **Add Address** button
3. Fill in the required fields:
   - **Street Address** (required)
   - **City** (required)
   - **State** (required)
   - **ZIP Code** (required)
   - **Nickname** (optional, e.g. "Home", "Office")
   - **Address Type** (Home, Apartment, Condo, etc.)
   - **Ownership** (Own or Rent)
   - **Start Date** (when you moved in)
4. Click **Save**

## Tips
- You can add multiple addresses to track services at each location
- Use nicknames to easily identify addresses
- The start date helps track how long you've been at each address
HelpArticle.excerpt: Step-by-step guide to adding an address to your account.

HelpArticle.title: Connecting Your Services
HelpArticle.content:
# Connecting Your Services

## What Are Services?
Services are any recurring accounts tied to your address — utilities (electric, gas, water), internet, insurance, subscriptions, gym memberships, etc.

## How to Add a Service
1. Go to **Services** from the sidebar
2. Click **Add Service**
3. Select or search for a **provider** from our database
4. Choose the **category** (Electric, Gas, Internet, etc.)
5. Link it to one of your **addresses**
6. Enter the **monthly cost** (optional but recommended for budgeting)
7. Set **billing date** and **contract end date** if applicable

## Service Categories
- **Utilities**: Electric, Gas, Water, Trash
- **Communication**: Phone, Internet, Cable
- **Insurance**: Auto, Home, Health, Life
- **Financial**: Bank, Credit Card, Mortgage
- **Lifestyle**: Gym, Subscriptions
- **Transportation**: Toll, Transit
HelpArticle.excerpt: How to add and manage utility and service providers.

HelpArticle.title: Creating a Moving Plan
HelpArticle.content:
# Creating a Moving Plan

## Overview
A Moving Plan helps you organize everything about your move — from packing to transferring services.

## Steps
1. Go to **Moving** from the sidebar
2. Click **Create Moving Plan**
3. Select your **from** and **to** addresses
4. Set your **move date**
5. ReloTrack will auto-generate a task checklist

## Managing Tasks
- Check off tasks as you complete them
- Add custom tasks for your specific needs
- Tasks are organized by category (Packing, Services, Documents, etc.)

## Box Tracking
- Track boxes by room (Kitchen, Bedroom, etc.)
- Mark boxes as packed
- See your overall packing progress

## Tips
- Start your plan at least 4-6 weeks before your move
- Use the task list as a checklist to avoid forgetting anything
- Transfer or cancel services as you check them off
HelpArticle.excerpt: Plan your move step by step with tasks and timelines.

HelpArticle.title: How to Transfer Services When Moving
HelpArticle.content:
# How to Transfer Services When Moving

## Before Your Move
1. **Review your services**: Go to Services and note which ones need to be transferred, cancelled, or replaced
2. **Contact providers early**: Most utility companies recommend 2-4 weeks notice
3. **Schedule disconnection**: Set a final date for your current address (usually your move-out date)
4. **Schedule connection**: Set a start date at your new address (ideally 1-2 days before move-in)

## Common Services to Transfer
- **Electric & Gas**: Contact your current provider or find a new one at your destination
- **Water**: Usually managed by the city/county — contact your new municipality
- **Internet/Cable**: Check if your provider serves your new area; if not, find a new one
- **Insurance**: Update your address with auto, home/renters, and health insurance providers

## Services That Follow You
These typically just need an address update:
- Phone carriers
- Streaming subscriptions
- Bank accounts
- Credit cards
- Gym memberships (check for location transfers)

## Don't Forget
- **USPS mail forwarding**: Set up at moversguide.usps.com
- **DMV**: Update your driver's license within the required timeframe
- **Voter registration**: Register to vote at your new address
HelpArticle.excerpt: Guide to transferring utilities and services to your new address.

HelpArticle.title: Tracking Monthly Service Costs
HelpArticle.content:
# Tracking Monthly Service Costs

## Service Cost Tracking
When adding or editing a service, enter the **monthly cost** field. This allows ReloTrack to:
- Show total monthly expenses per address
- Display spending by category on your dashboard
- Track cost trends over time

## Budget Feature
For more detailed tracking, use the **Budget** section:
1. Go to **Budget** from the sidebar
2. Add a monthly budget entry
3. Set planned vs. actual income and expenses
4. View category breakdowns
5. Compare budget vs. actual spending

## Dashboard Widgets
Your dashboard includes several cost-related widgets:
- **Spending by Address**: See how much each address costs monthly
- **Expenses by Category**: Pie chart of where your money goes
- **Upcoming Bills**: Bills due in the next 30 days
- **Top Spending**: Your most expensive services
HelpArticle.excerpt: Monitor your spending across all services and addresses.

HelpArticle.title: Comparing Service Providers
HelpArticle.content:
# Comparing Service Providers

## Provider Database
ReloTrack maintains a comprehensive database of service providers across all 50 states, including:
- Utility companies (electric, gas, water)
- Internet and cable providers
- Insurance companies
- Phone carriers
- And many more

## How Providers Work
- **Federal providers** are available nationwide (e.g., AT&T, GEICO)
- **State providers** serve specific states (e.g., PG&E in California)

## Community Reviews
Before choosing a provider, check out community reviews:
1. Go to **Community** from the sidebar
2. Search for a provider by name
3. Read reviews from other ReloTrack users
4. See average ratings and sub-ratings

## Writing Reviews
Help others by sharing your experience:
1. Click **Write a Review** in the Community section
2. Select the provider
3. Rate your experience (1-5 stars)
4. Add optional sub-ratings (customer service, pricing, reliability)
5. Write your review
6. Reviews are moderated for quality before publishing
HelpArticle.excerpt: Find and compare service providers for your area.

HelpArticle.title: Complete Moving Checklist
HelpArticle.content:
# Complete Moving Checklist

## 6-8 Weeks Before
- [ ] Create a moving plan in ReloTrack
- [ ] Research moving companies or rent a truck
- [ ] Start decluttering — sell, donate, or discard items you don't need
- [ ] Begin collecting packing supplies

## 4-6 Weeks Before
- [ ] Notify your landlord (if renting)
- [ ] Set up USPS mail forwarding
- [ ] Transfer or cancel utilities
- [ ] Update address with banks, insurance, employers
- [ ] Schedule movers or reserve truck

## 2-4 Weeks Before
- [ ] Start packing non-essential items
- [ ] Label boxes by room
- [ ] Confirm moving arrangements
- [ ] Plan travel to new home

## 1 Week Before
- [ ] Pack essentials box (first-night items)
- [ ] Finish packing
- [ ] Clean current home
- [ ] Confirm utility activation at new address

## Moving Day
- [ ] Do a final walkthrough of your old home
- [ ] Take meter readings
- [ ] Hand over keys
- [ ] Supervise loading/unloading

## After Moving
- [ ] Update driver's license at your new state's DMV
- [ ] Register to vote
- [ ] Explore your new neighborhood
- [ ] Update ReloTrack with your new address and services
HelpArticle.excerpt: Everything you need to do before, during, and after your move.

HelpArticle.title: Packing Tips & Box Management
HelpArticle.content:
# Packing Tips & Box Management

## General Packing Tips
- **Start early**: Begin packing rooms you use least
- **Label everything**: Mark boxes with room name and contents
- **Pack heavy items in small boxes**: Books, tools, etc.
- **Pack light items in large boxes**: Pillows, linens, etc.
- **Use towels and linens as padding**: Save on packing materials
- **Keep essentials separate**: Pack a first-night box with toiletries, chargers, change of clothes, snacks

## Box Tracking in ReloTrack
Your moving plan includes box management:
1. Open your moving plan
2. Add boxes with a label and room assignment
3. Mark boxes as packed as you go
4. Track your overall packing progress

## Room-by-Room Strategy
- **Kitchen**: Wrap dishes individually, pack glasses upright
- **Bedroom**: Use wardrobe boxes for hanging clothes
- **Bathroom**: Seal liquids in zip-lock bags
- **Office**: Back up electronics, label cables
- **Garage**: Drain gas from power tools
HelpArticle.excerpt: Efficient packing strategies and how to use box tracking.

HelpArticle.title: Moving Timeline: Week by Week
HelpArticle.content:
# Moving Timeline: Week by Week

## 8 Weeks Out
- Research your new area's service providers
- Get moving quotes from 3+ companies
- Create your moving plan in ReloTrack

## 6 Weeks Out
- Book your mover or reserve a truck
- Start notifying service providers of your move
- Begin decluttering

## 4 Weeks Out
- Start packing non-essential rooms
- Set up USPS mail forwarding
- Transfer or set up new utilities
- Update address with financial institutions

## 2 Weeks Out
- Confirm all moving arrangements
- Pack most rooms
- Arrange childcare/pet care for moving day
- Get cash for tips

## 1 Week Out
- Finish packing
- Prepare essentials box
- Clean and photograph old home
- Confirm utility disconnection/connection dates

## Moving Day
- Final walkthrough and meter readings
- Supervise movers
- Hand over keys

## Week After
- Unpack essentials first
- Update DMV, voter registration
- Explore your new neighborhood
- Update ReloTrack with new services
HelpArticle.excerpt: A structured timeline to keep your move on track.

HelpArticle.title: Managing Your Subscription
HelpArticle.content:
# Managing Your Subscription

## Available Plans
- **Free**: Basic move checklist, 3 addresses, 10 services, and Home Dossier preview.
- **Individual**: $24/year by default or $4.99/month, up to 10 addresses and 100 services.
- **Family**: $39/year by default or $7.99/month, up to 6 members, 15 addresses, and 500 services.
- **Pro**: $59/year by default or $11.99/month, up to 10 members, 25 addresses, 1,000 services, and 3 concurrent move plans.

## How to Change Your Plan
1. Go to **Settings** > **Subscription**
2. View your current plan and usage
3. Click **Upgrade** or **Downgrade**
4. Confirm your selection

## Billing
- Annual billing is the default/hero option; monthly billing is optional when offered.
- You can cancel anytime
- Downgrading takes effect at the end of your billing cycle
- No refunds for partial months
HelpArticle.excerpt: How to view, upgrade, or change your ReloTrack plan.

HelpArticle.title: Family Sharing & Permissions
HelpArticle.content:
# Family Sharing & Permissions

## Overview
Family Sharing lets multiple people collaborate on managing addresses, services, and moving plans.

## How to Invite Family Members
1. Go to **Family** from the sidebar
2. Click **Invite Member**
3. Enter their email address
4. Choose a role: **Admin**, **Editor**, or **Viewer**
5. Set specific permissions for what they can access

## Roles
- **Admin**: Full access — can add, edit, and delete everything
- **Editor**: Can add and edit, but cannot delete or manage members
- **Viewer**: Read-only access to shared data

## Permissions
You can grant granular access to:
- Addresses
- Services
- Moving Plans
- Budget
- Documents
HelpArticle.excerpt: Invite family members and manage their access.

HelpArticle.title: Exporting Your Data
HelpArticle.content:
# Exporting Your Data

## How to Export
1. Go to **Settings** > **Data Export**
2. Choose what to export:
   - Addresses
   - Services
   - Moving Plans
   - Documents
   - Budget data
3. Select format (CSV or JSON)
4. Click **Export**
5. Download will start automatically

## What's Included
- All your personal data
- Service details and costs
- Moving plan tasks and progress
- Budget entries
- Document metadata (not the files themselves)

## Data Portability
You have the right to your data at any time. Exports are available in standard formats that can be imported into spreadsheet applications.
HelpArticle.excerpt: Download your data for backup or transfer.

HelpArticle.title: Your Data Privacy Rights
HelpArticle.content:
# Your Data Privacy Rights

## Our Commitment
ReloTrack is committed to protecting your personal data. We follow industry best practices and comply with applicable privacy laws.

## Your Rights
- **Access**: View all data we have about you
- **Correction**: Update any incorrect information
- **Deletion**: Request complete deletion of your account and data
- **Export**: Download your data in standard formats
- **Opt-out**: Control notification and marketing preferences

## Data We Collect
- Account information (name, email)
- Addresses and service details
- Usage data for improving the experience
- We do NOT sell your data to third parties

## Security Measures
- All data encrypted in transit (TLS) and at rest
- Regular security audits
- Role-based access controls
- Automated threat detection
HelpArticle.excerpt: How we protect your data and your rights.

HelpArticle.title: How to Delete Your Account
HelpArticle.content:
# How to Delete Your Account

## Before You Delete
- Export your data first (Settings > Data Export)
- This action is **permanent** and cannot be undone
- All your data will be deleted including addresses, services, documents, and moving plans

## Steps
1. Go to **Settings**
2. Scroll to the **Danger Zone** section
3. Click **Delete Account**
4. Confirm by typing 'DELETE' in the confirmation dialog
5. Your account and all data will be permanently removed

## What Happens
- All personal data is deleted immediately
- Your reviews will be anonymized (not deleted)
- Shared family data will no longer be accessible to you
- You will be signed out and redirected to the home page

## Need Help?
If you're having issues with your account, consider reaching out to support first — we may be able to help resolve your concerns without deleting your account.
HelpArticle.excerpt: Steps to permanently delete your account and data.

FAQ.question: How do I create an account?
FAQ.answer: Click 'Get Started' on the landing page and sign up with your email address. You can also sign up using Google or other social providers through our authentication system.

FAQ.question: How do I change my email address?
FAQ.answer: Go to Settings > Profile and update your email address. You'll need to verify your new email before the change takes effect.

FAQ.question: Can I have multiple accounts?
FAQ.answer: We recommend using a single account with Family Sharing to manage multiple addresses. Each person should have their own account.

FAQ.question: How do I reset my password?
FAQ.answer: Click 'Forgot Password' on the login page. Enter your email address and we'll send you a password reset link.

FAQ.question: Is my data secure?
FAQ.answer: Yes. All data is encrypted in transit and at rest. We use industry-standard security practices including regular audits, role-based access controls, and secure authentication.

FAQ.question: How do I add a new service provider?
FAQ.answer: Go to Services > Add Service, then search our database of 300+ providers. If your provider isn't listed, you can add a custom entry.

FAQ.question: Can I track services at multiple addresses?
FAQ.answer: Yes! Add as many addresses as you need and link services to each one. Your dashboard will show a combined view of all your services.

FAQ.question: How do bill reminders work?
FAQ.answer: When you set a billing date on a service, ReloTrack will send you reminders via email and in-app notifications before the bill is due. You can customize reminder timing in Settings > Notifications.

FAQ.question: What service categories are available?
FAQ.answer: We support 20+ categories including: Electric, Gas, Water, Internet, Phone, Cable/TV, Insurance (Auto, Home, Health), Mortgage/Rent, Gym, Subscriptions, and more.

FAQ.question: How far in advance should I create a moving plan?
FAQ.answer: We recommend creating your plan 6-8 weeks before your move date. This gives you enough time to handle all the tasks like transferring services, packing, and notifying providers.

FAQ.question: Can I share my moving plan with family?
FAQ.answer: Yes! Use Family Sharing to invite members who can view or help manage the moving plan. Each person can check off their assigned tasks.

FAQ.question: What tasks does ReloTrack auto-generate?
FAQ.answer: Based on your addresses and services, we generate tasks for: USPS mail forwarding, DMV updates, voter registration, utility transfers, insurance updates, and more. You can also add custom tasks.

FAQ.question: Can I have multiple active moving plans?
FAQ.answer: Yes, you can create and manage multiple moving plans simultaneously. This is useful if you're managing moves for different family members or properties.

FAQ.question: What's included in the free plan?
FAQ.answer: The free plan includes: up to 2 addresses, 10 services, 1 moving plan, basic budget tracking, and community access. Upgrade to Pro for unlimited everything.

FAQ.question: How do I cancel my subscription?
FAQ.answer: Go to Settings > Subscription and click 'Cancel Plan'. Your access continues until the end of your billing period. You can resubscribe anytime.

FAQ.question: Do you offer refunds?
FAQ.answer: We offer a 14-day money-back guarantee on annual plans. Monthly plans are non-refundable but you can cancel anytime to stop future charges.

FAQ.question: How do I delete my account?
FAQ.answer: Go to Settings > Danger Zone > Delete Account. This permanently removes all your data. We recommend exporting your data first from Settings > Data Export.

FAQ.question: Do you sell my data?
FAQ.answer: No, never. We do not sell, rent, or share your personal data with third parties for marketing purposes. Your data is only used to provide the ReloTrack service.

FAQ.question: Can I export my data?
FAQ.answer: Yes! Go to Settings > Data Export to download your addresses, services, moving plans, and budget data in CSV or JSON format.

FAQ.question: How long do you keep my data?
FAQ.answer: We keep your data as long as you have an active account. If you delete your account, all data is permanently removed within 30 days. Anonymized analytics data may be retained.
